input: Neural Network Training Process: 1) Forward Propagation → make prediction, 2) Calculate Loss → how wrong are we?, 3) Backpropagation → calculate gradients, 4) Gradient Descent → update weights, 5) Repeat. Loss Functions: MSE for regression (predicting numbers), Cross-Entropy for classification (predicting categories). Backpropagation: going backward through network to calculate how much each weight contributed to error. Gradient = rate of change of loss w.r.t. each weight. Gradient Descent formula: parameter_new = parameter_old + learning_rate × gradient(loss). Learning rate: too small = slow/stuck, too large = overshoots, just right = efficient. Variants: GD (all data, slow, smooth), SGD (1 random point, fast, noisy), Mini-batch (32-128 samples, balanced, most common), Adam (adaptive per-parameter, most popular).
